title: The Siege of Kamarjah
description: Sababah or Shabibah, the mawli of Qays b. 'Abdallah al-Bahili, came to the troops who were encamped at Kamarjah.2A5 Kamarjah measuring 7,508.33 English feet or 1.43 English miles. See Hava, al-Fard'id alDurriyyah, s.v. m-y-1, dh-r-'. However, there were many different measurements fora dhird 242. Text: Ghazak, read: Ghurak, as Codd. and throughout the text. 243. This may be identical with the fortified village of Afshinah, west of Bukhara. See Barthold, Turkestan, ttg. 244. Outside the walls of Bukhara. See Gibb, Arab Conquests, 70. 245.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 25
